data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 1:00 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was little changed at 77,676, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 declined 0.03% to 23,520, as gain in IT shares offset the impact of worries about corporate earnings.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee was marginally lower versus the U.S. dollar at 85.89, heading into the U.S. jobs report that will likely decide whether the dollar remains on a bullish path.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 100.19 rupees, with the yield little changed at 6.7603%, as traders awaited a debt auction result.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was unchanged at 6.50%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rose 1 bp to 6.21%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 6.20%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.71%.

data.title: YIT to Lead Development Phase of Turku Tramway Depot Project in Finland
data.description:
YIT said Friday that it was picked by the Turku Tramway Alliance to manage the planning phase of the Turku tramway depot project in Finland.

The Finnish construction group will lead the planning of the Port of Turku-Varissuo tramway line's depot and power supply station buildings, focusing on cost-effective strategies and identifying target and roof prices.

The target budget is estimated at 30 million euros and will be finalized during the development stage, after which Turku Tramway Alliance will decide on the implementation phase, subject to the Turku City Council's investment decision at the end of 2025.

data.title: Japan futures post weekly loss on subdued demand prospects
data.description:
Japanese rubber futures edged up for a third straight session on Friday, aided by a softer yen and firmer raw material prices, although they were on track to end the week lower on a subdued demand outlook from top consumer China.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for June delivery , closed up 3.9 yen, or 1.07%, at 368.6 yen ($2.33) per kg. The contract fell 1.58% for the week.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for March delivery rose 155 yuan, or 0.92%, to 16,980 yuan ($2,315.69) per metric ton. It lost 0.53% this week.

  • The dollar has gained 0.5% on the yen this week to buy 158.405 yen.

  • A weaker currency makes yen-denominated assets more affordable to overseas buyers.

  • In the physical market, raw material prices are seeing some correction, said Farah Miller, founder of independent rubber-focused data firm Helixtap Technologies.

  • The northeast monsoon will weaken with isolated heavy rains in the lower south from Jan. 14 to 16, top producer Thailand's meteorological agency said.

  • "The initial dip in prices was due to the spillover effect of the weakness in Chinese stocks. However, there was some rebound...after technical support levels were hit," Miller added.

  • China stocks slipped on Thursday as official data underscored persistent deflationary pressure despite fresh government consumption stimulus.

  • Meanwhile, global economic growth is projected to be held back by the top two economies, the U.S. and China, remaining at 2.8% in 2025, according to a United Nations report.

  • The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for February delivery last traded at 190.6 U.S. cents per kg, up 1.5%.

  • Japan's financial markets will be closed on Monday for a public holiday. Trading will resume on Tuesday, Jan. 14.

($1 = 158.3200 yen)

($1 = 7.3326 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Ziccum AB (publ) files for bankruptcy
data.description:
The board of directors of Ziccum AB (publ) (”Ziccum”or the ”Company”) has today, 10 January 2025, resolved to file for bankruptcy on behalf of the Company to manage the financial situation that has occurred. The application for bankruptcy will be submitted to Lund District Court.

The decision is based on a thorough assessment of alternatives, considering the current financial situation, and the board has reached the overall conclusion that the Company does not have enough financing to fulfil outstanding obligations and continue operations. The Company completed a rights issue in fourth quarter 2024 where the proceeds gained were significantly lower than expected. Meanwhile, other sources of financing are judged inadequate to maintain continued Company operations, as the current investor Global Corporate Finance (“GCF) has chosen to decline the Company’s request for continued financing according to the existing agreement.

Considering the above, the board of directors of Ziccum has today decided to file for bankruptcy at Lund District Court.

Ziccum regrets the inability of the Company to pursue its continued technology and business development based on LaminarPace®, its patented pharmaceutical technology platform.

data.title: Sainsbury's Christmas Sales Grew on Higher Volumes, Expects Profit in Line With Market Views
data.description:
(07:00 GMT) *Sainsbury's: Wins Market Share at Christmas For the Fifth Consecutive Year
(07:01 GMT) *Sainsbury's 3Q Grocery Sales Up 4.1%
(07:02 GMT) *Sainsbury's Christmas Grocery Sales Up 3.8%
(07:03 GMT) *Sainsbury's 3Q Total Retail Sales (Exc. Fuel) Up 2.7%
(07:03 GMT) *Sainsbury's 3Q LFL Sales Up 2.8%
(07:04 GMT) *Sainsbury's Christmas Sales Up 3.8%
(07:05 GMT) *Sainsbury's 3Q General Merchandise and Clothing Sales Down 0.1%
(07:05 GMT) *Sainsbury's Christmas General Merchandise and Clothing Sales Up 3.4%
(07:06 GMT) *Sainsbury's Sees FY25 Retail Adj Oper Pft in Line With Market Views
(07:08 GMT) *Sainsbury's Sees FY25 Retail Adj Oper Pft at Midpoint of GBP1.01B-GBP1.06B Guidance
(07:08 GMT) *Sainsbury's: Target Reflects Leverage From Grocery Volume Growth
(07:09 GMT) *Sainsbury's Sees FY25 Financial Services Adj Operating Pft at Around GBP30M
(07:09 GMT) *Sainsbury's Had Seen FY25 Financial Services Adj Operating Pft between GBP15M-GBP25M
(07:09 GMT) *Sainsbury's Continues to See FY25 Retail Free Cash Flow of At Least GBP500M
(07:11 GMT) *Sainsbury's Sees Strong Growth in Nectar360 Revenues as Retail Media Income Grows
(07:12 GMT) *Sainsbury's to Deliver at Least GBP100M Incremental Profit in the 3Y to FY26/27
(07:15 GMT) *Sainsbury's To Increase Salaries of Hourly-Paid Workers by 5% in the Year Ahead
(07:37 GMT) Sainsbury's Christmas Sales Grew on Higher Volumes, Expects Profit in Line With Market Views

By Michael Susin

J Sainsbury reported sales volume growth for the key Christmas period and said it expects to deliver retail operating profit for its fiscal year in line with market consensus.

The British grocer on Friday said sales excluding fuel rose 2.7% in the 16 weeks ended Jan. 4 compared with the same period a year earlier. Like-for-like sales rose 2.8%.

Both overall sales and grocery sales during the Christmas period rose 3.8%, marking the seventh consecutive quarter of food volume growth.

Sales of general merchandise and clothing grew 3.4%, with the Argos business posting an increase of 1.1%.

The FTSE 100-listed company said it now expects retail underlying operating profit--the company's preferred metric, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--for the year ending March at the midpoint of its guidance of 1.01 billion to 1.06 billion pounds ($1.24 billion and $1.30 billion). This is in line with market expectations of 1.03 billion pounds, according to a company-compiled consensus.

The company upgraded its guidance for total financial services underlying operating profit to around 30 million pounds, up from the previous guidance of 15 million and 25 million pounds. Retail free cash flow guidance remains unchanged at no less than 500 million pounds.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: FTSE 100 Seen Opening Lower Ahead of U.S. Jobs Data — Market Talk
data.description: The FTSE 100 is expected to open 15 points lower, or 0.2%, according to IG. The index closed up 68 points, or 0.8%, to 8319 points on Thursday. A weaker sterling boosted the large proportion of overseas earners listed on the index Thursday. Sterling fell following a sharp rise in U.K. government bond yields amid concerns about rising U.K. borrowing and a weak U.K. economic outlook. Investors are now looking ahead to the U.S. nonfarm payrolls report at 1330 GMT for clues on the Federal Reserve's interest rate path. Stronger-than-expected data would push back expectations for another rate cut until June, while softer-than-forecast data would strengthen bets for a May cut, Swissquote Bank's Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a note. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Direct Line Target Raised to 275.00p From 250.00p by Deutsche Bank
Direct Line Cut to Hold From Buy by Deutsche Bank
Ifo: German Residential Construction Sector Slump Continues at 2024-end
data.description:
Germany's residential construction industry remained under pressure in December 2024, despite marginal improvement in business conditions, an ifo Institute survey showed Friday.

The proportion of surveyed companies reporting order shortage eased slightly to 53.6% in December 2024 from 54% in the prior month. Companies were also less pessimistic about their current business situation and future outlook.

However, ifo Head of Surveys Klaus Wohlrabe warned that Germany continues to face "major challenges" in addressing the need for living space.

data.title: Romania Retail Sales Growth at 3-Month Low in November
data.description:
Retail sales in Romania rose by 9.2% year-on-year in November 2024, easing from a 10.6% growth in the previous month.

This marked the softest growth in retail activity since August, as sales slowed for food, beverages, and tobacco (4.3% vs 5.1% in October) and non-food products (15.2% vs 18.8%).

Meanwhile, trade increased further for automotive fuel (3.2% vs 2.2%).

On a monthly basis, retail trade grew by 0.4%, following a 0.9% gain in the preceding period.

data.title: Changes in the Management Team of Wetteri Oyj
data.description:
Wetteri Oyj

Wetteri Plc Stock exchange release 10 January 2025 at 9.30

Mika Rissanen, Car Dealership Manager in Joensuu, has been appointed as a member of Wetteri Plc's Executive Management Team. Rissanen will be responsible for the company's heavy vehicle business following Antti Ollikainen's departure from Wetteri and the Executive Management Team after the completion of the Wetteri Power Oy transaction on January 1, 2025.

More information

Aarne Simula, CEO and President, Wetteri Plc

Tel. +358 400 689 613, aarne.simula@wetteri.fi

Wetteri Plc - an entrepreneur-driven growth company in the automotive sector

Wetteri Plc is an entrepreneur-driven growth company in the automotive sector. The company engages in the retail sales of passenger cars, commercial vehicles and heavy vehicles, and produces maintenance and repair shop services for vehicles, from passenger cars to heavy vehicles. Headquartered in Oulu, the company has 50 locations in Finland. The company employs app. 1,000 people, of whom around 76% work in maintenance and repair services. Wetteri promotes digitalisation in the automotive sector and is an important player on the common journey towards emission-free motoring. More information: sijoittajat.wetteri.fi/en/

https://news.cision.com/wetteri-oyj/r/changes-in-the-management-team-of-wetteri-oyj%2Cc4089843

data.title: Glenveagh Properties Posts Higher FY24 Profit, Revenue
data.description:
Glenveagh Properties (GVR.IR) said Friday its profit before tax and revenue for 2024 rose year over year.

Pretax profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 113 million euros, compared with 55 million euros earlier. EPS moved to 0.17 euro from 0.08 euro.

The Irish homebuilder's revenue was 869 million euros, compared with 608 million euros a year ago.

Looking ahead, Glenveagh Properties expects to deliver an EPS of 0.195 euro for 2025.

data.title: Peab Bags SEK207 Million Construction Contract for Barracks in Sweden
data.description:
Peab (PEAB-B.ST) said Friday it secured the contract worth 207 million kronor to build new barracks in Visby, Sweden.

The Swedish Fortifications Agency is commissioning the project, which involves the construction of a 6,500-square-meter barracks building for use by the Swedish Armed Forces.

The Swedish construction company will begin work on the project in January 2025, with completion expected by May 2026.

data.title: Bitcoin (BTC) Supply Shock Unlikely in 2025: Here’s Why
data.description:
With Bitcoin’s increasing presence in traditional financial systems and speculation around a US BTC strategic reserve, some experts foresee a major supply shock during this cycle, potentially disrupting the 4-year cycle theory with extraordinary price growth.

However, a new report indicates that such a supply shock is unlikely to happen in 2025.Analyzing Bitcoin Long-Term Holder (LTH) Supply

While Bitcoin’s halving, rising institutional interest, and the introduction of spot BTC ETFs in the US have amplified discussions of constrained supply, detailed data suggests otherwise, according to a report by CEX.IO, shared with CryptoPotato. The combination of long-term holder (LTH) activity, ETF activity, and evolving liquidity trends indicates a strong supply ecosystem that is capable of mitigating potential shocks.

Key to this assessment is the behavior of LTH supply post-halving. Historically, halving events trigger a notable transition of coins from LTH to short-term holders (STH), thereby increasing market liquidity. In 2024 alone, LTH supply dominance fell by 9%, releasing 1.58 million BTC into the market.

With an average 16% decline in LTH dominance observed during previous post-halving cycles, a projected transfer of 1.4 million BTC from LTH to STH is expected in 2025. The report explained that this ensures that increased demand from institutions or governments will likely be met by substantial LTH profit-taking, tempering supply constraints.ETF Dynamics, OTC Activity, and Market Liquidity

ETFactivity, often cited as a potential driver of supply shocks, also appears less impactful upon closer examination. Despite US spot Bitcoin ETFs amassing over 1.13 million BTC in 2024, much of this accumulation stemmed from cash-and-carry trades rather than direct directional investments. These arbitrage strategies, which are reliant on derivatives like CME futures, balance supply and demand without directly pressuring spot markets.

Additionally, ETFs currently account for less than 4% of Bitcoin’s total trading volume, which further reduces their capacity to drive a systemic supply imbalance.

Market liquidity and exchange reserves also play crucial roles, as detailed by CEX.IO. While exchange-held Bitcoin reservesdroppedto record lows in 2024, withdrawals largely signaled transfers to cold storage rather than liquidation, which reflected long-term confidence.

Simultaneously, OTC platforms increased their holdings by over 200,000 BTC, which points to a redistribution of liquidity rather than outright depletion. This diversification, coupled with stable daily transfer volumes, indicates a balanced and active market.

Finally, market depth metrics reveal improving liquidity conditions. The growing resilience is depicted by the rise in USD-denominated liquidity by 61% in 2024 despite reduced BTC-denominated depth. With larger exchanges consolidating market share and US platforms expanding dominance, the liquidity landscape appears to be well-positioned to handle increased demand in 2025.

Together, these factors reinforce the conclusion that Bitcoin’s supply remains strong, making a significant supply shock unlikely in the coming year. Instead, the report states that the market is poised for measured growth within the established 4-year cycle framework.

data.title: US Job Growth Seen Slowing in December
data.description:
The US economy is expected to add 160K jobs in December 2024, reflecting a slowdown from the 227K added in November when the labor market rebounded from the impact of Boeing strikes and disruptions caused by Hurricanes Helene and Milton.

The unemployment rate is projected to remain steady at 4.2%.

Meanwhile, wages are forecast to rise by 0.3% month-over-month, slightly below November’s 0.4% increase, keeping annual wage growth steady at 4%.

This would bring total payroll gains for 2024 to 2.144 million, the lowest since the pandemic year of 2020 and below the 3.01 million added in 2023.

Despite the slowdown, the figures continue to signal a strong and stable labor market.

data.title: Sweden Industrial Output Rebounds to 2.6%
data.description:
Industrial production in Sweden rose by 2.6% year-on-year in November 2024, rebounding from a 2% fall in the previous month.

This marked the first growth in industrial activity since March, as output recovered for mining and quarrying (0.6% vs -4.9% in October), manufacturing (2.6% vs -1%), and electricity, gas, steam and hot water plants (3.3% vs -1.9%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial output rose by 2.4% in November, the most since April 2023, and rebounding from an upwardly revised 0.9% drop in the preceding month.

data.title: Nordic Stocks Tipped to Open Slightly Lower — Market Talk
data.description: Nordic markets are seen opening slightly lower, with IG calling the OMXS30 down 0.1% at around 2547. Markets were quiet Thursday with the U.S. partially closed in honor of ex-president Jimmy Carter, SEB analysts say in a note. European stock markets generally closed in positive territory, while Asian stock markets have ticked lower this morning ahead of Friday's U.S. jobs data. Futures indicate a tentative opening in both the U.S. and Europe. "The direction of the U.S. labor market is crucial for the Federal Reserve, and any unexpected weakness (or strength) could trigger significant market reactions," SEB says. OMXS30 closed at 2549.58, OMXN40 at 2536.42 and OBX at 1379.37. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0734 GMT - Chinese shares fell, with all sectors closing in the red, led by losses in consumer-related stocks. Investor sentiment remained weak after China's consumer prices posted a modest 0.2% growth in 2024, reflecting ongoing deflationary pressures. Midea Group fell 3.0% and Gree Electric Appliances dropped 2.3%. In the consumer services sector, China Tourism Group Duty Free Corp. lost 1.7%, while Shanghai Jinjiang International Hotels declined 3.1%. Among the few gainers, Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corp. rose 2.0% and SAIC Motor Corp. added 1.3%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index ended 1.3% lower at 3168.52, the Shenzhen Composite Index fell 2.2% and the ChiNext Price Index declined 1.8%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0721 GMT - BBVA's reduction of the acceptance level of its $12 billion-plus hostile bid to take over Banco de Sabadell is mostly a technical adjustment at the request of Spanish regulators, RBC Capital Markets' Benjamin Toms and Pablo de la Torre Cuevas say in a research note. BBVA said its offer requires more than half of the effective voting rights of Sabadell, excluding treasury shares. Regulators saw a need to clarify what BBVA's acceptance threshold was and the Spanish bank's move effectively means the minimum acceptance is reduced by 0.7%, according to RBC. "We continue to be of the view that this deal gets done, probably by [the first half], and probably with some relatively minor remedies and a 10% increase in the offer price," the analysts say. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0705 GMT - Airbus missed its plane delivery target for 2024 but the European plane maker's deliveries should start to pick up in the second quarter of the year, Citi analysts Charles J Armitage and Sam Burgess say in a research note. Airbus said Thursday that it had delivered 766 commercial aircraft to customers in 2024, beating the previous year's figure, but short of the 770 it had guided for. "We believe 1Q 2025 deliveries are likely to be weak, due to few aircraft in inventory following the strong December," the analysts say. They expect deliveries to pick up in 2Q as new LEAP engine blades are built and put into new engines. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0651 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi edged 0.2% lower to close at 2515.78, snapping a five-session winning streak. The index rose 3.0% for the week. Foreign and institutional investors were net sellers in choppy trading. Losses in battery and defense stocks outweighed gains in auto shares. EV battery maker LG Energy Solution fell 2.8% after forecasting a 4Q operating loss. Guided-missile maker LIG Nex1 dropped 5.3%. Meanwhile, Hyundai Motor rose 6.1% after announcing a partnership with Nvidia to develop artificial-intelligence solutions for future mobility. USD/KRW settled 0.3% higher at 1,465.00 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield was up 3.8 bps at 2.816%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0650 GMT - Airbus's year-end results are positive for its 2025 outlook, but engine deliveries are a key factor for the company's prospects this year, RBC Capital Markets' Ken Herbert says in a research note. The European plane maker delivered 123 aircraft in December to end the year with 766 shipments to customers, short of the 770 the company targeted but ahead of consensus expectations of 755. "We believe the key bottleneck to greater A320 [narrow-body jet] deliveries in 2024 was the engine supply chain, with the Leap in particular," the analyst says, referring to the engine made by the Safran-GE Aerospace joint venture CFM International. RBC expects Airbus to guide for around 825 deliveries in 2025, which would be positive for investor sentiment, the analyst says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0648 GMT - Japanese stocks ended lower, as auto and pharmaceutical stocks led declines amid growing concerns of higher borrowing costs. Otsuka Holdings dropped 4.2% and Toyota Motor lost 2.4%. Fast Retailing fell 6.5% after weaker 1Q profit from China cast doubt on its outlook. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield climbed 2.5 basis points to 1.195%, its highest level since May 2011. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 1.0% to 39190.40. Investors await U.S. jobs data due later in the day. USD/JPY is at 158.36, compared with 158.14 as of Thursday 5 p.m. Eastern time. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0641 GMT - Tata Consultancy Services is likely benefiting from some tailwinds, HDFC Securities analysts say in a research report, as they maintain the stock's add rating. These tailwinds include the Indian IT services provider's strong total contract value of $10.2 billion, driven by big deal wins across various markets and industries, the analysts say. Management's outlook of calendar 2025 growth appears to be better than calendar 2024, supported by an increase in successful deployment of generative artificial intelligence engagements, the analysts add. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to INR4,590.00 from INR4,545.00, based on 28x estimated FY 2027 EPS, above five-year average of 27x. Shares are 6.1% higher at INR4,283.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0634 GMT - Amorepacific Corp. may report solid 4Q earnings backed by a narrower loss in China, Mirae Asset Securities analyst Bae Song-yi writes in a note. Bae expects the South Korean cosmetics maker to post an operating profit of KRW84 billion for the October-December quarter, quadrupling from a year earlier. Progress in business restructuring in China likely led the company to narrow operating losses from its Chinese operations to KRW20 billion in 4Q from KRW30 billion in 3Q and KRW40 billion in 2Q, she reckons. Mirae Asset raises its target for the stock by 14% to KRW160,000 and keeps a buy rating. Shares are 0.7% higher at KRW116,200. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0545 GMT - Zydus Lifesciences' earnings are poised to rise on higher sales contribution from Sitagliptin 505b(2) drug, Nomura analysts say in a research report. Assuming Sitagliptin market at $300-$500 million at a discounted price, the opportunity size for Zydus Lifesciences could be as big as $100-$150 million annualized, the analysts say. The brokerage lifts its earnings estimates for the Indian pharmaceutical company by 1% for FY 2025, 6% for FY 2026, and 6% for FY 2027. It raises the stock's rating to buy from neutral and the target price to INR1,140.00 from INR1,030.00. Shares are 0.4% higher at INR1,013.10. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0532 GMT - Tencent's overall business fundamentals and growth outlook remain intact despite recently being added to a Pentagon list with alleged Chinese military ties, OCBC analysts write in a note. However, the news may indicate rising geopolitical tensions and could be a share-price overhang until further clarity on the development, they say. Tencent's key businesses are value-added services and online advertising, which remain positive over the long term. The recent share-price selloff may be an overreaction and provides for a long-term accumulation opportunity, they say. OCBC maintains a buy rating on the stock at fair value of HK$560.00. Shares are last at HK$373.00. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0506 GMT - New Zealand's S&P/NZX 50 closed 0.4% lower at 12895.98. The benchmark index is down 1.6% early in the new year, running on thin volumes and little stock-specific news during the Southern Hemisphere's summer holiday season. The index was weighed by losses in financial and telecom stocks in Friday's session. Westpac posted the biggest daily fall, shedding 2.1%. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Chinese Shares Fall, Consumer Stocks Lead Losses — Market Talk
data.description: Chinese shares fell, with all sectors closing in the red, led by losses in consumer-related stocks. Investor sentiment remained weak after China's consumer prices posted a modest 0.2% growth in 2024, reflecting ongoing deflationary pressures. Midea Group fell 3.0% and Gree Electric Appliances dropped 2.3%. In the consumer services sector, China Tourism Group Duty Free Corp. lost 1.7%, while Shanghai Jinjiang International Hotels declined 3.1%. Among the few gainers, Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corp. rose 2.0% and SAIC Motor Corp. added 1.3%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index ended 1.3% lower at 3168.52, the Shenzhen Composite Index fell 2.2% and the ChiNext Price Index declined 1.8%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: UBS Group to Redeem $1.25 Billion of Tier 1 Notes
data.description:
Swiss bank UBS Group plans to redeem its $1.25 billion of 7% tier 1 capital notes on Feb. 19.

The notes, issued in February 2015, will be redeemed on the first call date, according to a Friday release.

data.title: Shanxi Huhua Group to Buy Back Up to 20 Million Yuan Worth of Shares
data.description:
Shanxi Huhua Group approved a share repurchase plan, targeting 633,334 to 666,666 shares for at least 19 million yuan up to 20 million yuan, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The shares make up about 0.32%-0.33% of the company's total equity, the filing said.

The buyback is capped at 30 yuan per share and is set for 12 months.

The shares will support employee stock plans or equity incentives.

data.title: Alliance Pharma Accepts $430.4 Mln Takeover From Largest Shareholder DBAY Advisors
data.description:
By Ian Walker

Alliance Pharma said it agreed to a 349.7 million pound ($430.4 million) takeover by its largest shareholder DBAY Advisors.

Under the deal, accepting shareholders of the medicine and healthcare-product supplier will get 62.5 pence in cash for each share held, the companies said Friday. The price is a 41% premium to the company's closing price of 44.35 pence on Thursday.

Accepting shareholders can also elect to receive unlisted shares as an alternative to the cash offer.

DBAY Advisors, together with funds under management or advisement, currently own 151.1 million Alliance Pharma shares, or 27.9% of its issued share capital.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: TVT Digital Technology Completes Setting Up Subsidiary
data.description:
Shenzhen TVT Digital Technology completed the establishment of a subsidiary after receiving a business license, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

The goods trading subsidiary, Shenzhen Guanyuan Import and Export Trading, has a registered capital of 5 million yuan, the Chinese video security products manufacturer said.

The company's shares closed less than 4% lower.

data.title: Norway Inflation Rate Falls to 4-Year Low
data.description:
The annual consumer inflation rate in Norway fell to 2.2% in December 2024, the lowest level since December 2020, down from 2.4% in November and approaching the central bank’s 2% target.

The main downward pressure came from lower inflation in food and non-alcoholic beverages (4% vs. 4.2% in November) and housing and utilities (1.9% vs. 2.1%).

Additionally, there was a significant easing in costs for furnishings, household equipment, and routine maintenance (0.5% vs. 2.5%) as well as recreation and culture (3.1% vs. 4.3%).

Meanwhile, the CPI adjusted for tax changes and excluding energy products (CPI-ATE) rose by 2.7% year-on-year in December, slowing from a 3% increase in November.

On a monthly basis, the CPI declined by 0.1%, defying expectations of a 0.1% rise and following a 0.3% increase in November.

For the full year, the headline inflation rate averaged 3.1%, the lowest in four years.

data.title: China vice finance minister says to expect more proactive fiscal policy this year
data.description:
• We can expect more proactive fiscal policy in 2025, in terms of strength, efficiency and timing
  • To speed up fiscal spending in a bid to formulate actual spending
  • China's fiscal policy has abundant policy room and tools
  • China has relatively big room of raising debt and deficit

data.title: Bocom International Buys $4.9 Million of HSBC Bonds
data.description:
Bocom International unit Preferred Investment Management acquired $4.9 million of bonds issued by HSBC in the over-the-counter market for nearly $5 million, a Thursday bourse filing said.

The bonds make up part of an issue of $500 million floating rate senior unsecured notes falling due in 2030.

The bonds have been acquired for investment purposes.

data.title: Sai Silks (Kalamandir) Opens New Store in Trichy, India
data.description:
Sai Silks (Kalamandir) has opened a new store at Trichy in Tamil Nadu, India on Friday, according to a same-day disclosure to the stock exchanges.

This is the company's 67th store under the format of Kanchipuram Varamahalakshmi Silks.

Shares of the company were down 1% in recent trade.
